releases.label: HICAT
releases.summary: The HIPASS Catalogue — the largest uniform catalogue of HI sources compiled to its date, with 4,315 sources identified purely by their HI content from the southern region (declination < +2 deg) of HIPASS, the first blind HI survey to cover the entire southern sky (Parkes 64-m multibeam receiver, rms 13 mJy/beam, velocity range -1,280 to 12,700 km/s). 99% complete at a peak flux of 84 mJy (Zwaan et al. 2004). Served via the ATNF HIPASS data release interface; cubes also in CASDA.

releases.label: NHICAT
releases.summary: Northern extension of the HIPASS catalogue covering +2 < dec < +25.5 deg (including the Virgo Cluster region): 1,002 sources with v_hel > 300 km/s (the -300 to +300 km/s range excluded to avoid Galactic emission). Together with HICAT, the entire HIPASS survey found 5,317 galaxies identified purely by their HI content, over 71% of the sky.
